metadata.name: OSDOCR
metadata.summary: Toolkit for ocr of old structured documents

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.information_extraction.journal_template_to_text
documentation: Converts ocr_results to text using journal_template

functions.name: OSDOCR.preprocessing.image.create_waifu2x_model
documentation: Create Waifu2x model

functions.name: OSDOCR.preprocessing.image.run_waifu2x
documentation:
Run Waifu2x to clean or upscale image.

Args:
    target_image (str): path to image
    method (str, optional): method to use ['scale2x','scale4x',autoscale,'noise']. Defaults to 'scale2x'.
    model_type (str, optional): model type ['photo','art','art_scan']. Defaults to 'photo'.
    noise_level (int, optional): noise level [-1 (None),0,1,2,3]. Defaults to 1.
    result_image_path (str, optional): path to save result. Defaults to None, in which case result will be saved to same directory.

functions.name: OSDOCR.preprocessing.image.identify_document_images_layoutparser
documentation:
Identify document image elements from image, using layout parser.
Identified image elements (old document):
- Photograph
- Illustration
- Map
- Comics/Cartoon
- Editorial Cartoon

Identified image elements (new document):
- Table
- Figure

functions.name: OSDOCR.preprocessing.image.remove_image_blocks
documentation: Remove blocks from image

functions.name: OSDOCR.preprocessing.image.identify_document_images
documentation: Identify document images from image

functions.name: OSDOCR.preprocessing.image.remove_document_images
documentation: Remove document images from document. Image blocks will be saved in save_blocks_path folder in file "blocks.json"

functions.name: OSDOCR.preprocessing.image.fix_illumination
documentation:
Use model from HVI_CIDNet (https://github.com/Fediory/HVI-CIDNet) to fix illumination.

Split image parameter is used to improve time of processing, althoug for some models it might create different results (as they won't receive the whole picture as input).

Available model weights (https://onedrive.live.com/?authkey=%21ACob5LruXUFfRwY&id=2985DB836826D183%21346&cid=2985DB836826D183):
- best_SSIM
- best_PSNR
- LOL-Blur
- SICE
- SID
- w_perc

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_fix.remove_empty_boxes
documentation: Removes empty boxes from ocr results.

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_fix.block_bound_box_fix
documentation:
Fixes block bounding boxes. Deals with intersections of boxes and overlapping boxes.  
Args:
    ocr_results (OCR_Tree): ocr results
    text_confidence (int, optional): confidence of text, used for finding empty blocks. Defaults to 10.
    find_delimiters (bool, optional): if True, try to find delimiters using rules, else checks for block type. Defaults to True.
    find_images (bool, optional): if True, try to find images using rules, else checks for block type. Defaults to True.
    
Returns:
    OCR_Tree: ocr results cleaned

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_fix.text_bound_box_fix
documentation: Fix bound boxes of blocks with text by reducing their sides when confidence of side text is too low.

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_fix.bound_box_fix
documentation:
Fix bound boxes

Mainly overlaping boxes

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_fix.bound_box_fix_image
documentation:
Fix bound boxes of blocks by pixel analysis of bounding boxes in image.

For each direction (left, right, top, bottom) we check (iterating on the interesting direction) 
for the first black pixel that has neighbouring black pixels.

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_fix.unite_blocks
documentation: Unite same type of blocks if they are horizontally aligned and adjacent to each other

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_fix.delimiters_fix
documentation: Fix delimiters. Adjust bounding boxes, and remove delimiters inside text.

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_fix.remove_solo_words
documentation: Remove boxes with single words that are inside of other boxes.

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_fix.find_text_titles
documentation:
From ocr_results, searches within text blocks for titles and separatest them from the rest.
Potential titles should be lines that are taller than normal text size, and come after closed text, or are in the beggining of the block.

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_fix.split_block
documentation: Splits block into new blocks, based on delimiter and cut direction. Adjust text inside blocks to fit new area.

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_fix.split_whitespaces
documentation:
If a block contains sequence of whitespaces of ratio 'dif_ratio' compared to normal word distance, split the block into two blocks.

Only possible if block has single line, or every line has similar amount of whitespaces in the same position.

classes.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree.OCR_Tree_load_error
documentation: Exception to handle OCR_Tree load errors

classes.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree.OCR_Tree

methods.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree.OCR_Tree.__init__
documentation:
Initialize ocr_tree object

Available constructors:

ocr_tree() - default empty constructor

ocr_tree(level:int, page_num:int, block_num:int, par_num:int, line_num:int, word_num:int, box:Box, text:str='',conf:int=-1,id=None,type:str=None)

ocr_tree(json_list:list[dict])

ocr_tree(json_file:str)

documentation: Convert ocr_tree to hocr

documentation: Copy ocr_tree object

documentation: Check if attribute is value

documentation: Add child to ocr_results

methods.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree.OCR_Tree.reset_level
documentation: Reset level of ocr_results

methods.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree.OCR_Tree.id_boxes
documentation:
Id boxes in ocr_results

Args:
    * level (list[int], optional): Levels to id. Defaults to [2].
    * ids (list, optional): List of ids. 
        * Works as a tuple of size 2. 
        * First position is greatest id, second is dict with number of occurences of each id. 
        * Defaults to None.
    * delimiters (bool, optional): If False, only id non delimiters. Defaults to True.
    * area (Box, optional): Area to id boxes. Defaults to None.
    * override (bool, optional): If True or has no id, override id. Defaults to True.

methods.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree.OCR_Tree.clean_ids
documentation:
Clean ids in ocr_results

Args:
    level (list[int], optional): Levels to clean. Defaults to [2].

',3:'

Get boxes in area

methods.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree.OCR_Tree.type_color
documentation:
Get block color, in bgr value, based on type

Colors:
    - text: yellow
    - image: black
    - title: red
    - highlight : purple
    - delimiter: blue
    - caption : white
    - other: green

Get blocks below

documentation: Get average confidence of boxes in level

methods.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree.OCR_Tree.update_position
documentation:
Update position of box and children.

Arguments:
    top (int, optional): Top position. Defaults to None.
    left (int, optional): Left position. Defaults to None.
    absolute (bool, optional): If True, positions are absolute, else values are added to current position. Defaults to False.

methods.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree.OCR_Tree.update_size
documentation: Update size of box and children.

methods.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree.OCR_Tree.update_box
documentation:
Update box and children positions.

Arguments:
    left (int, optional): Left position. Defaults to None.
    right (int, optional): Right position. Defaults to None.
    top (int, optional): Top position. Defaults to None.
    bottom (int, optional): Bottom position. Defaults to None.
    children (bool, optional): If True, adjust coordinate instead of replacing. Defaults to False.

methods.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree.OCR_Tree.scale_dimensions
documentation: Scale dimensions of box and children.

documentation: Get max bottom of tree

methods.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree.OCR_Tree.min_left

Class to represent OCR results as a tree object

modules.name: OSDOCR
documentation: Script to run OSDOCR

functions.name: OSDOCR.gui.osdocr_gui.layouts.extra_layout.build_gui_config_pipeline
documentation: Build window for config ocr pipeline

functions.name: OSDOCR.gui.osdocr_gui.layouts.main_layout.build_gui_main
documentation: Build GUI

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_analyser.get_text_sizes
documentation:
Get text sizes from ocr_results using peak detection on line size frequency


Frequency graph is smoothened using chosen method.

Available methods:
    - WhittakerSmoother
    - savgol_filter

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_analyser.get_columns
documentation:
Get columns from ocr_results using peak detection on box left margin frequency


Frequency graph is smoothened using chosen method.

Available methods:
    - WhittakerSmoother
    - savgol_filter

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_analyser.get_journal_areas
documentation:
Get areas of journal (header, body, footer) based on line box top margin frequency.


Frequency graph is smoothened using chosen method.

Available methods:
    - WhittakerSmoother
    - savgol_filter

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_analyser.analyze_text
documentation:
Analyse text from ocr_results and return text data as dict

Tries to find info about normal text size, number of lines and number of columns


### Result dict:
- normal_text_size: normal text size of the document page
- <other text sizes>* : other text sizes of the document if any (need to be found as peaks by get_text_sizes)
- columns: list of columns as Box class

- average_word_distance: average distance between words
- average_character_width: average character width

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_analyser.estimate_journal_header
documentation:
Estimate journal header blocks and dimensions

Main focus on pivoting using potential delimiters

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_analyser.join_aligned_delimiters
documentation: Join aligned delimiters

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_analyser.estimate_journal_columns
documentation:
Estimate journal columns blocks and dimensions

Main focus on pivoting using potential delimiters

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_analyser.estimate_journal_template
documentation: Tries to estimate a journal's template, such as header and different columns

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_analyser.draw_journal_template
documentation: Draw bounding boxes on journal image of path 'image_path'

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_analyser.draw_bounding_boxes
documentation:
Draw bounding boxes on image of type MatLike from cv2

Return image with bounding boxes

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_analyser.draw_articles
documentation:
Draw articles in image


Choose a unique color for each article

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_analyser.next_top_block
documentation:
Get next top block

Estimates block with best potential to be next top block

Uses top and leftmost blocks for reference

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_analyser.calculate_reading_order_naive
documentation:
Calculate reading order of ocr_tree of block level.

Order left to right, top to bottom.

Naive approach: only takes into account boxes position, not taking into account context such as article group


Returns ocr_results with id reordered

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_analyser.next_top_block_context
documentation:
Get next top block

Estimates block with best potential to be next top block, using context such as block type as reference

If current_block is given, uses it as further reference

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_analyser.calculate_reading_order_naive_context
documentation:
Calculate reading order of ocr_tree of block level.

Order left to right, top to bottom.

Naive approach: only takes into account boxes position, not taking into account context such as article group

Context approach: takes into account context such as caracteristics of blocks - title, caption, text, etc


Returns: ocr_tree with id reordered

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_analyser.categorize_box
documentation:
Categorize single block into a type.

Args:
    target_block: block to categorize
    blocks: list of blocks
    block_analysis: dictionary resulting from analyze_text. Needs at least normal_text_size as key.
    conf: text confidence level

Returns block type

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_analyser.categorize_boxes
documentation:
Categorize blocks into different types

Types:
- 1: Delimiter
- 2: Title
- 3: Caption
- 4: Text
- 5: Image
- 6: Table
- 7: Other

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_analyser.topologic_graph
documentation:
Generate topologic graph of blocks


Return:
- topological graph: for each block, Node object with list of blocks that come after it

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_analyser.sort_topologic_order
documentation:
Sort topologic graph into list


Searches for first block in topologic graph, that has no blocks before it in order map, that are not already in order list

For next blocks, in case of ties, chooses block with greatest attraction


If sort_weight is True, sorts blocks by weight, otherwise sorts by order of appearance. Narrows parents before sorting.

Return:
- order_list: list of block ids in order

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_analyser.topologic_order_context
documentation:
Generate topologic order of blocks


Context approach: takes into account context such as caracteristics of blocks - title, caption, text, etc

Context will allow to estimate attraction between blocks, such as title blocks being very atracted to non title blocks

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_analyser.calculate_block_attraction
documentation:
Calculate attraction between blocks


Attraction is calculated based on block's characteristics such as type, size, position, etc


0: No attraction

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_analyser.graph_isolate_articles
documentation: Isolate articles in topologic graph

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_analyser.order_ocr_tree
documentation: Calculates level 2 OCR Tree reading order and returns these blocks in an ordered list

functions.name: OSDOCR.ocr_tree_module.ocr_tree_analyser.extract_articles
documentation: Extract articles from document using OCR results and image information. Articles are assumed to only be in body segment of target.

functions.name: OSDOCR.pipeline.save_articles

functions.name: OSDOCR.pipeline.binarize_image

functions.name: OSDOCR.pipeline.run_target_split
documentation:
Segment target into Header, Body, Footer and run OCR on each part. 
Body is further split into columns.

Final OCR results are then merged into single ocr_tree.

functions.name: OSDOCR.pipeline.run_target_image
documentation:
Run pipeline for single image.
- Image preprocessing
- OCR
- Convert to ocr_tree

Return ocr_tree and image path (in case of preprocessing)

functions.name: OSDOCR.pipeline.clean_ocr
documentation: Clean ocr_tree

functions.name: OSDOCR.pipeline.unite_ocr_blocks
documentation: Unite ocr_tree

functions.name: OSDOCR.pipeline.restore_document_images
documentation: Restore document images

functions.name: OSDOCR.pipeline.identify_images_ocr_results
documentation: Identify images in ocr_results

functions.name: OSDOCR.pipeline.find_title_blocks
documentation: From ocr_results, searches within text blocks for titles and separatest them from the rest

functions.name: OSDOCR.pipeline.run_target
documentation:
Run pipeline for single target.

- A : image
1. Analyze target 
2. Preprocess image
3. OCR
4. Convert to ocr_tree


- B : hOCR/json
1. Convert to ocr_tree

- General
1. Clean ocr_tree
2. Analyse ocr_tree
3. Extract articles
4. Save articles

Places element provided into a Column element so that its placement in the layout is retained.
\:param elem: the element to put into the layout
\:return: A column element containing the provided element

functions.name: OSDOCR.gui.aux_utils.utils.collapse
documentation:
Helper function that creates a Column that can be later made hidden, thus appearing "collapsed"
\:param layout: The layout for the section
\:param key: Key used to make this seciton visible / invisible
\:param visible: Whether the section should be visible by default
\:param vertical_alignment: The vertical alignment of the section
\:return: A pinned column that can be placed directly into your layout
\:rtype: sg.pin

methods.name: OSDOCR.aux_utils.box.Box.distance_to
documentation:
Get distance to box

Uses euclidean distance between center points of boxes

If border arg is initialized, uses distance between borders instead of center points.
Border arg can be one of: 'left','right','top','bottom','closest'

If border arg is None, uses center points of boxes

If border arg is 'closest', uses the closest border to the center point of box or closest border of box.
    * range_x and range_y can be used to specify the range when checking if boxes are within each other

methods.name: OSDOCR.aux_utils.box.Box.center_point
documentation: Get center point of box

methods.name: OSDOCR.aux_utils.box.Box.top_middle_point
documentation: Get top middle point of box

documentation: Get bottom middle point of box

methods.name: OSDOCR.aux_utils.box.Box.left_middle_point
documentation: Get left middle point of box

methods.name: OSDOCR.aux_utils.box.Box.right_middle_point
documentation: Get right middle point of box

methods.name: OSDOCR.aux_utils.box.Box.distance_to_point
documentation: Get distance to point

methods.name: OSDOCR.aux_utils.box.Box.vertices
documentation:
Get vertices of box.
Order:
    - top left
    - top right
    - bottom right
    - bottom left

methods.name: OSDOCR.aux_utils.box.Box.closest_edge_point
documentation:
